zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder'於1222年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾圖特波利時，她的父親Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby30歲，母親Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby21歲。她於1244年與Sir William de Vesci在英國諾圖波藍德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1290年5月11日在英國越可爾斯卡波洛享年68歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾斯卡波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder'于1222年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾圖特波利时，她的父亲Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby30岁，母亲Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby21岁。她于1244年与Sir William de Vesci在英国諾圖波藍德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1290年5月11日在英国越可爾斯卡波洛享年68岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾斯卡波洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: 'The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Elder'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: distinguishing nickname from Middle English and Older Scots elder ‘parent; older, senior person’, perhaps in some cases denoting the older of two bearers of the same personal name. It may have been used also to denote an elder of a church, a senior and respected member of the congregation. Compare Senior .
English: in southeastern England, this may be a variant of   Helder  .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Elter  , which has the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Senior
meanings: from Middle English seignour ‘lord’ (Old French seignor), either for someone who was a lord (for example, of a manor), or perhaps a nickname for a peasant who emulated his lord.
distinguishing nickname for the elder of two bearers of the same personal name (for example, a father and son or two brothers), from Middle English, Latin senior ‘elder’.
English:

variants.name: Elter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Reinhold.
German:

distinguishing nickname bestowed on the elder (Middle High German elter) of two bearers of the same personal name or for an alderman in a community. In North America, this surname has frequently been assimilated to English and Scottish Elder .

variants.name: Elders
meanings: English (North eastern England): variant of Elder 1 and 2, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized patronymic from Eldert, from the ancient Germanic personal name Adelhard, composed of the elements adel ‘noble’ and hard ‘hardy, strong’, a vowel variant of Alders .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' was born in 1222, in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, was 30 and her mother, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, was 21. She married Sir William de Vesci in 1244, in Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 11 May 1290, in Scarborough, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' est née en 1222, à Tutbury, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Vesci en 1244, à Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 mai 1290, à Scarborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Scarborough, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' 1222 in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby 30 und ihre Mutter Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby 21. Sie hat 1244 in Northumberland, England Sir William de Vesci geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. Mai 1290 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Scarborough, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' è nata il nel 1222 nel Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Vesci nel 1244, nel Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 11 maggio 1290, nel Scarborough, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Scarborough, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Scarborough, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアスカーボロウ
ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder'が1222年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアトゥトブリーで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derbyは30歳，母Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derbyは21歳であった。1244年にイギリスノースウンバーランドでSir William de Vesciと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1290年5月11日にイギリスヨークシュアイアスカーボロウで68歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアスカーボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder'(이)가 1222년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 튜트버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1244년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 Sir William de Vesci(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1290년 5월 11일 영국 요르크실르 스카버루에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 스카버루에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' nasceu em 1222, em Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William de Vesci em 1244, em Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 11 de maio de 1290, em Scarborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Scarborough, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' родилась в 1222 г. в местности тутбури, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Vesci в 1244 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 11 мая 1290 г. в местности скарбороую, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности скарбороую, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Ferrers 'The Elder' nació en 1222, en Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir William de Vesci en 1244, en Northumberland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 11 de mayo de 1290, en Scarborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Scarborough, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
